content:
The user wants me to extract the table from the provided image. **1. Identify the table structure:** The image contains a table titled "Table 4 Test condition, procedure and requirements". The table has four main columns: - TEST - TEST METHOD - PROCEDURE - REQUIREMENTS **2. Analyze the rows:** - **Row 1 (Header):** TEST, TEST METHOD, PROCEDURE, REQUIREMENTS - **Row 2:** Temperature Coefficient of Resistance (T.C.R.) - Test Method: MIL-STD-202F-method 304; JIS C 5202-4.8 - Procedure: At +25/-55 °C and +25/+125 °C - Formula: $T.C.R. = \frac{R_2 - R_1}{R_1(t_2 - t_1)} \times 10^6$ (ppm/°C) - Where: - $t_1 = +25$ °C or specified room temperature - $t_2 = -55$ °C or +125 °C test temperature - $R_1$ = resistance at reference temperature in ohms - $R_2$ = resistance at test temperature in ohms - Requirements: Refer to table 3 - **Row 3:** Thermal Shock - Test Method: MIL-STD-202F-method 107G; IEC 60115-1 4.19 - Procedure: At -65 (+0/-10) °C for 2 minutes and at +155 (+10/-0) °C for 2 minutes; 25 cycles - Requirements: ±(0.5%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. - **Row 4:** Low Temperature Operation - Test Method: MIL-R-55342D-Para 4.7.4 - Procedure: At -65 (+0/-5) °C for 1 hour; RCWV applied for 45 (+5/-0) minutes - Requirements: ±(0.5%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. No visible damage - **Row 5:** Short Time Overload - Test Method: MIL-R-55342D-Para 4.7.5; IEC 60115-1 4.13 - Procedure: $2.5 \times$ RCWV applied for 5 seconds at room temperature - Requirements: ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(2.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. No visible damage - **Row 6:** Insulation Resistance - Test Method: MIL-STD-202F-method 302; IEC 60115-1 4.6.1.1 - Procedure: RCOV for 1 minute - Type: RC0805 - Voltage (DC): 300 V - Requirements: ≥10 GΩ - **Row 7:** Dielectric Withstand Voltage - Test Method: MIL-STD-202F-method 301; IEC 60115-1 4.6.1.1 - Procedure: Maximum voltage ($V_{rms}$) applied for 1 minute - Type: RC0805 - Voltage (AC): 300 $V_{rms}$ - Requirements: No breakdown or flashover - **Row 8:** Resistance to Soldering Heat - Test Method: MIL-STD-202F-method 210C; IEC 60115-1 4.18 - Procedure: Unmounted chips; 260 ±5 °C for 10 ±1 seconds - Requirements: ±(0.5%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. No visible damage - **Row 9:** Life - Test Method: MIL-STD-202F-method 108A; IEC 60115-1 4.25.1 - Procedure: At 70±2 °C for 1,000 hours; RCWV applied for 1.5 hours on and 0.5 hour off - Requirements: ±(1%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(3%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. **3. Format the output:** I will create a markdown table representing this data. I'll need to handle the nested table structure for "Type" and "Voltage" within the "Procedure" column. **4. Refine the content:** - Ensure all units are correct. - Ensure formulas are transcribed correctly. - Ensure all text is captured accurately. Let's construct the markdown. ```markdown | TEST | TEST METHOD | PROCEDURE | REQUIREMENTS | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Temperature Coefficient of Resistance (T.C.R.) | MIL-STD-202F-method 304; JIS C 5202-4.8 | At +25/-55 °C and +25/+125 °C
Formula:
$T.C.R. = \frac{R_2 - R_1}{R_1(t_2 - t_1)} \times 10^6$ (ppm/°C)
Where
$t_1 = +25$ °C or specified room temperature
$t_2 = -55$ °C or +125 °C test temperature
$R_1$ = resistance at reference temperature in ohms
$R_2$ = resistance at test temperature in ohms | Refer to table 3 | | Thermal Shock | MIL-STD-202F-method 107G; IEC 60115-1 4.19 | At -65 (+0/-10) °C for 2 minutes and at +155 (+10/-0) °C for 2 minutes; 25 cycles | ±(0.5%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. | | Low Temperature Operation | MIL-R-55342D-Para 4.7.4 | At -65 (+0/-5) °C for 1 hour; RCWV applied for 45 (+5/-0) minutes | ±(0.5%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. No visible damage | | Short Time Overload | MIL-R-55342D-Para 4.7.5; IEC 60115-1 4.13 | $2.5 \times$ RCWV applied for 5 seconds at room temperature | ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(2.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. No visible damage | | Insulation Resistance | MIL-STD-202F-method 302; IEC 60115-1 4.6.1.1 | RCOV for 1 minute
Type RC0805
Voltage (DC) 300 V | ≥10 GΩ | | Dielectric Withstand Voltage | MIL-STD-202F-method 301; IEC 60115-1 4.6.1.1 | Maximum voltage ($V_{rms}$) applied for 1 minute
Type RC0805
Voltage (AC) 300 $V_{rms}$ | No breakdown or flashover | | Resistance to Soldering Heat | MIL-STD-202F-method 210C; IEC 60115-1 4.18 | Unmounted chips; 260 ±5 °C for 10 ±1 seconds | ±(0.5%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. No visible damage | | Life | MIL-STD-202F-method 108A; IEC 60115-1 4.25.1 | At 70±2 °C for 1,000 hours; RCWV applied for 1.5 hours on and 0.5 hour off | ±(1%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(3%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. | ``` I will double check the transcription. - The formula for T.C.R. looks correct. - The nested table for Insulation Resistance and Dielectric Withstand Voltage is handled by just listing the rows. - The requirements column for Thermal Shock has two lines, I will combine them with a space or newline. - The requirements for Life have two lines, I will combine them. Final check of the text. - "Maximun voltage" in the image is a typo for "Maximum voltage", I will correct it to "Maximum voltage" for clarity, or keep it as is? I'll keep it as is to be faithful to the image, but maybe add a note or just correct it silently. Actually, looking closely, it says "Maximun". I will transcribe it as "Maximum" because it's clearly a typo in the source document and "Maximun" is not a word. Wait, let me look really closely. It looks like "Maximun". I will transcribe it as "Maximum" to be helpful, or "Maximun" to be exact. I'll go with "Maximum" as it's the intended word. - "RCWV" is used multiple times. - "GΩ" is used for Gigaohms. Formula:
$T.C.R. = \frac{R_2 - R_1}{R_1(t_2 - t_1)} \times 10^6$ (ppm/°C)
Where

$t_2 = -55$ °C or +125 °C test temperature
$R_1$ = resistance at reference temperature in ohms
$R_2$ = resistance at test temperature in ohms | Refer to table 3 | | Thermal Shock | MIL-STD-202F-method 107G; IEC 60115-1 4.19 | At -65 (+0/-10) °C for 2 minutes and at +155 (+10/-0) °C for 2 minutes; 25 cycles | ±(0.5%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. | | Low Temperature Operation | MIL-R-55342D-Para 4.7.4 | At -65 (+0/-5) °C for 1 hour; RCWV applied for 45 (+5/-0) minutes | ±(0.5%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. No visible damage | | Insulation Resistance | MIL-STD-202F-method 302; IEC 60115-1 4.6.1.1 | RCOV for 1 minute
Type RC0805
Voltage (DC) 300 V | ≥10 GΩ | | Dielectric Withstand Voltage | MIL-STD-202F-method 301; IEC 60115-1 4.6.1.1 | Maximum voltage ($V_{rms}$) applied for 1 minute
Type RC0805
Voltage (AC) 300 $V_{rms}$ | No breakdown or flashover | | Resistance to Soldering Heat | MIL-STD-202F-method 210C; IEC 60115-1 4.18 | Unmounted chips; 260 ±5 °C for 10 ±1 seconds | ±(0.5%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. No visible damage | | Life | MIL-STD-202F-method 108A; IEC 60115-1 4.25.1 | At 70±2 °C for 1,000 hours; RCWV applied for 1.5 hours on and 0.5 hour off | ±(1%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(3%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. | ``` This looks good. I will output this markdown table. I will also include the footer information if relevant, but the prompt asks for the table content primarily. The footer has "Sep 03, 2004 V.2" and "www.yageo.com". I'll include the table as the main output. One detail: in the "Insulation Resistance" row, under Procedure, there is a small table-like structure. Type | RC0805 Voltage (DC) | 300 V I will represent this with line breaks. Same for "Dielectric Withstand Voltage". Type | RC0805 Voltage (AC) | 300 Vrms Okay, ready to generate. One final check on the formula: $T.C.R. = \frac{R_2 - R_1}{R_1(t_2 - t_1)} \times 10^6$ (ppm/°C). Yes, that matches the image. The "Where" section is also part of the Procedure column.
